item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Only possible criticism is, why would the Foundation put any effort at all in suppressing knowledge of this fabulous turkey? It's so old and folktale-esque no civilian could prove anything. But then I got to thinking, they don't want any non-Foundation scientists finding a trail of evidence to follow that leads them to discovering Sarkicism or thaumaturgy. And containment could easily be achieved with a low-level agent or two just keeping an eye on civilian archeological investigations in the region, something they can probably handle on top of many more of their other duties. My only possible criticism has just been resolved.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this, +1 for sure.

Things Which Sparked Joy:
- Short, but compelling. All satisfying meat, no filler.
- Good prose, perfectly immersive and felt like an actual file the Foundation would keep for a long-dead anomaly.
- Fabulous characterization, Corneliu is best boi/best girl

Also, I listened to All Star by Smash Mouth while reading part of this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I get what you're going for with having a more benign Sarkic SCP, but I cannot see this as an anomaly worthy of an SCP designation. We have a ton of folk tales that are just that, tales. They don't all warrant Foundation involvement.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love it wyd, really funny and has a good narrative. But can you change the meleagris gallopavo to Meleagris gallopavo so that it is correctly formatted as a scientific name. It irks me more than it should. Thanks B.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alright, I changed the last footnote to make things a bit less cryptic. Not really a big deal, I was debating between this result and the previous one, in which the Foundation found nothing. If there's any opposition to this, please let me know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Looking back on it I might redo the footnote at the end stating that nothing was found. I was going for kinda a sad story, but a bunch of people are pointing out that it takes away more than it adds.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed this, I think the character writing in the addendum is great and the sudden elevation of a turkey to celebrity is a funny turn.

I agree with the above comments about it not being justifiably anomalous. The last footnote is the crux of the problem: Why not make it to where they find some sort of physical evidence of Corneliu in a house that corroborates the anecdotes? That would solve the issue. What is the lack of findings decided on here doing but making this more cryptic?

On that note,

is to be perpetuated as a local folk tale in the region of Ukraine where it was discovered.

…requires no Foundation involvement, as a local folk tale is what this already is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah ok, so that makes two people :0

I’ll keep it in mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What do you mean?

Edit: I was told this is in reference to SCP-1732. Are you suggesting I copied it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I prefer the SCP about the talking lion that was also a Roman emperor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seconding that this doesn't seem remotely anomalous; there isn't nearly enough to suggest that this would be something that merits documentation as an SCP. The connection to Sarkicism, likewise, is wholly superficial - the only connection it seems to have is a namedrop.

I'd be more welcoming of this if there was at least something substantial present in the article: some kind of evidence or residue or clear tells indicating that this event did actually occur, and more cultural substance to the documents that contributes to/elaborates on this aspect of Sarkicism.

All in all, I don't feel that there's enough meat to this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a fair observation. The intent behind this SCP object was to portray a more benign element of Sarkic history, but you are correct in pointing out that the anomalies are more evident in the events surrounding Cornelius than the bird himself. I'll definitely keep that in mind for the future, especially since ClicheCon is coming up! I'm very happy you found the article charming, and I'll always do my best to make sure that my stories are fun to read :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I personally like the article and find it really charming, I’m a bit confused about what the actual anomaly here is. Sure, it’s vaguely Sarkic and if proven true, would definately count as an anomalous thing. However all it is now is a legend that isn’t really prove able since we can’t find a body.

What seperates this story as being anomalous while tons of myths and legends exist with similar depictions of anomalies?

+1, anyway though. You made an entertaining read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good article that is short in a good way. Interesting to know a bit more about the sarkics, specially when they help others like 2815

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAH YEAH I FORGOT ABOUT THAT

edit: is this my curse? to be reminded of the blatant mistake of my first scp forever? i tremble but i will bear it with dignity

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You should probably replace "XXXX" with "3581"

Edit: Oh never mind. It was fixed. This comment can be deleted

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
This is far from my first submission to the wiki, but it is my first SCP, so that's pretty cool! Thanks to Gabriel Jade, Uncle Nicolini, and RockTeethMothEyes for taking a look at this. The character of Corneliu is based off an OC I made when I was 12, and I'll explain more on my author page.

Edit: Also I changed the file size so don't yell at me please!!!!! I'm already screaming at myself!

Edit 2: oh yeah the image is mine, i painted that earlier today
